story: My alarm yanks me into the cold. I open Outlast; my cohort’s death counter rockets—millions a minute—while 2020 looks normal. News shows outages and jammed lines. I call Hailey; her app matches mine. Someone pounds her door; I tell her not to open; she hangs up. Rattled afterward, Hailey changes course, heads for my building, and minutes later reaches my floor; her 'I'm coming up' text drops in the jam, and she stops right outside my studio door. I pack to flee west. The tally blasts past seventy million. My device vibrates: a proximity alert outside my studio. I freeze, hand on the knob, listening.
implausible_knowledge: Rattled afterward, Hailey changes course, heads for my building, and minutes later reaches my floor; her 'I'm coming up' text drops in the jam, and she stops right outside my studio door.
question: When the device buzzes with a proximity alert and the narrator’s hand is on the door, what will the narrator most likely do?
answer_with_knowledge: Call out to Hailey and open the door to pull her inside.
answer_without_knowledge: Stay silent, keep the door closed, and back away from it.
